note: Mathlib/Topology/MetricSpace/Similarity.lean:41 -- `def Similar (v₁ : ι → P₁) (v₂ : ι → P₂) : Prop := ∃ r : ℝ≥0, r ≠ 0 ∧ ∀ i₁ i₂, edist (v₁ i₁) (v₁ i₂) = r * edist (v₂ i₁) (v₂ i₂)`; docstring: 'the equivalence between indexed sets of points in a metric space where all corresponding pairwise distances have the same ratio' -- this IS the geometric-similarity concept (Q254465), specialized to triangles in Mathlib/Geometry/Euclidean/Similarity.lean.

Mathlib/MeasureTheory/OuterMeasure/Defs.lean:51 -- `structure OuterMeasure (α : Type*) where` with countable-subadditivity field, matching the standard measure-theory definition of an outer measure (a monotone, countably subadditive set function with measure of empty set 0). Direct exact match.

Mathlib/Algebra/MonoidAlgebra/Defs.lean:76 -- `structure MonoidAlgebra (R M : Type*) [Semiring R] where`; file docstring line 19 explicitly: 'The "group ring" ℤ[G] or the "group algebra" k[G] are typical uses' of this construction, instantiated when M is a group. MonoidAlgebra is strictly more general (works for any monoid, even magma via liftMagma) than the group-ring concept, so generalization not exact.

Mathlib/Geometry/Manifold/Bordism.lean:110 -- `structure SingularManifold.{u} (X : Type*) [TopologicalSpace X] (k : WithTop ℕ∞) ...`, file titled '(Unoriented) bordism theory', docstring defines bordant/bordism explicitly but states this file only builds the singular-manifold precursor; the bordism relation itself and bordism groups are named as future-PR TODOs (see lines 82-88: 'define bordisms and prove basic constructions', 'define the bordism relation and prove it is an equivalence relation'). So the cobordism relation/group is NOT yet formalized -- only its building block is. related, not exact/generalization.
